content.problemStatement:
In this task, you will create a program that asks the user to describe their dream pet.

The program will gather details such as the type of pet, its name, its color, and the number of pets the user dreams of having. 

Additionally, it will calculate what it would be like to have one more than their dream number of pets.
constraints: All strings have length ≤ 100 and contain no whitespace
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 100), the number of test cases. For each test case four lines follow: 1) pet_type – a string without spaces, 2) pet_name – a string without spaces, 3) pet_color – a string without spaces, 4) number_of_pets – a non‑negative integer (0 ≤ number_of_pets ≤ 1000).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output two lines. First line: "Your dream pet is a <pet_color> <pet_type> named <pet_name>." Second line: "You dream of having <number_of_pets> <pet_type>(s), but imagine having <total_pets>! How adorable!" where <total_pets> = <number_of_pets> + 1. The output of consecutive test cases is separated by a single newline character.

sampleTestCases.output: Your dream pet is a Yellow Parrot named Kiwi.\nYou dream of having 2 Parrot(s), but imagine having 3! How adorable!
sampleTestCases.explanation: The pet type is Parrot, name Kiwi, color Yellow, and the user wants 2 pets. Adding one gives 3.

sampleTestCases.output: Your dream pet is a white Cat named Whiskers.\nYou dream of having 3 Cat(s), but imagine having 4! How adorable!
sampleTestCases.explanation: Type Cat, name Whiskers, color white, desired count 3 → total 4.

sampleTestCases.output: Your dream pet is a Brown Dog named Buddy.\nYou dream of having 0 Dog(s), but imagine having 1! How adorable!
sampleTestCases.explanation: Zero desired pets, adding one results in 1.

hidden.label: minimum boundary
hidden.expectedOutput: Your dream pet is a Blue Fish named Nemo.\nYou dream of having 0 Fish(s), but imagine having 1! How adorable!

hidden.label: maximum boundary
hidden.expectedOutput: Your dream pet is a Green Parrot named Polly.\nYou dream of having 1000 Parrot(s), but imagine having 1001! How adorable!

hidden.label: edge case
hidden.expectedOutput: Your dream pet is a white Cat named M.\nYou dream of having 1 Cat(s), but imagine having 2! How adorable!

hidden.label: general case 1
hidden.expectedOutput:
Your dream pet is a Brown Dog named Buddy.
You dream of having 3 Dog(s), but imagine having 4! How adorable!
Your dream pet is a Grey Rabbit named Thumper.
You dream of having 5 Rabbit(s), but imagine having 6! How adorable!

hidden.label: general case 2
hidden.expectedOutput:
Your dream pet is a Golden Hamster named Nibbles.
You dream of having 2 Hamster(s), but imagine having 3! How adorable!
Your dream pet is a Red Parrot named Polly.
You dream of having 0 Parrot(s), but imagine having 1! How adorable!
Your dream pet is a Black Snake named Sly.
You dream of having 7 Snake(s), but imagine having 8! How adorable!

solution.approach: Read the integer T. Loop T times and for each iteration read the four values in the given order. The strings are single tokens, so simple token‑based input works. Construct the two required sentences using the read values; the second sentence needs the value number_of_pets+1. Print the two lines, adding a newline between test cases (but not after the last one). The algorithm is O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case is processed in constant time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are stored regardless of T.

code.Python3:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().splitlines()
    if not data:
        return
    t = int(data[0].strip())
    idx = 1
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(t):
        pet_type = data[idx].strip(); idx += 1
        pet_name = data[idx].strip(); idx += 1
        pet_color = data[idx].strip(); idx += 1
        number = int(data[idx].strip()); idx += 1
        out_lines.append(f"Your dream pet is a {pet_color} {pet_type} named {pet_name}.")
        out_lines.append(f"You dream of having {number} {pet_type}(s), but imagine having {number+1}! How adorable!")
    sys.stdout.write("\n".join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
